title: Season Piece: Cut 3
description: Re-stitch a spinning seasonal relic by tapping its shifting slices back in the pulsing order before the pattern tears apart.

howToPlayJson.title: Season Piece: Cut 3
howToPlayJson.objective: Memorize and replay increasingly fast pulse sequences across seasonal piece-slices, surviving sudden season shifts while building combo for a leaderboard score.

controls.outcome: Inputs that slice in sequence and plays its musical cue

controls.input: Press and hold the center seam button
controls.outcome: Banks the current combo through one incoming season shift
Begins a new 2-4 minute session immediately

steps: A broken circular relic appears as four large touch slices: Spring, Summer, Autumn, and Winter.
The game plays a short pulse pattern with distinct drum or chime sounds on the slices.
The player taps the slices back in order to repair the relic one seam at a time.
After every successful round, the pattern gains one beat and the playback speed increases slightly.
Every few seconds a 'season cut' twist triggers: two slices swap positions, so the player must follow sound-and-symbol identity instead of a
Use the center stitch hold once per round to protect combo through a swap, but holding too early leaves later beats exposed.
A wrong tap, missed beat, or late input tears the relic and ends the run instantly.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Score comes from repaired beats plus a combo multiplier that rises with flawless rounds. Perfect replays after a season cut give a bigger multiplier jump. Leaderboard play is simple high score per run.
tips: Learn each season by sound first, not screen position.
Save the stitch hold for longer patterns with a late swap.
Perfect rounds are worth more than rushing the first beat.
Watch the icons on each slice when the cut happens; the board moves, the identity does not.
